stem: An investment account was opened with an initial value of $890. The value of the account doubled every 10 years. Which equation represents the value of the account M(t), in dollars, t years after the account was opened?
explanation: Doubling every 10 years means multiplying by a factor of 2 once for each 10-year period, and t/10 counts those periods. Starting from $890, the value is M(t) = 890(2)^(t/10).
